content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Connie Barton
    Legal
    State Lands Commission
    1807 Thirteenth Street
    Sacramento, CA 95814

Date : Mar. 25, 1977

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Ted Prim

Subject:

     Attached please find a revised copy of our advice concerning

reporting obligations which may be incurred in connection

with the Western States Land Commission's convention. The

revision necessitated by 1 FPPC Ops. 110 is noted in the

memorandum by underlining and strikeout.

     If you have any further questions please call me.


TP:jo

Attachment

State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                    Date : Mar. 25, 1977


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Ted Prim

Subject: Advice to Connie Barton of State Lands Commission on December
         16, 1976.

The Western Association of State Land Commissions is composed of Commissioners and executive personnel from state land commissions from a number of western states. Each year the Association holds a convention at which its members discuss common problems of state land commissions. Since the Association is comprised of public members only, there are no funds available to sponsor such a convention. Therefore, the Association wishes to solicit the private sector for contributions of money, wine, food, and other supplies.

With respect to persons filing Statements of Economic Interests, I urged her to consult both the provisions of her agency's conflict of interest code and Article 2 of Chapter 7. If an official receives gifts of food, beverage, entertainment, etc. totalling $25 or more from all of the donors cumulatively, the filer should report the gift providing a brief description of the gift received, a good faith estimate of the value and a list of all the donors. The filer need not receive gifts of $25 or more from a single donor to trigger the reporting obligation. A filer would not, however, be disqualified from making or participating in a decision unless the filer received $250 or more in value from an individual donor. Thus, in the case of disqualification, the gifts are not cumulated among the various donors.

To be safe, I advised her that lobbyists should not be involved in making, arranging for the making, or acting as an intermediary or agent in the making of the above-described gifts. If an employer of a lobbyist or an §6108(b) filer furnished gifts to the Association of $25 or more, such items should be reported as payments to influence pursuant to

File
P.2
Mar. 25, 1977


Section 86109(c) and the officials who benefit from such
payments should be listed. An-employer-need-not-list-the
names-of-officials-benefitting-from-the-payments-unless
an-individual-pro-rata-share-of-the-payments-from-that
employer-totalled-$25-or-more.

I offered to review their solicitation and filing instruc-
tions, and she indicated that she would probably take ad-
vantage of this offer.


TP:jo

sections.question_synthetic: What are the reporting obligations for state officials who receive gifts of money, food, wine, and other supplies from private sector donors in connection with the Western Association of State Land Commissions' annual convention?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Officials must report cumulative gifts totaling $25 or more from all donors combined, providing a description, value estimate, and donor list; disqualification from decisions applies only when an individual donor provides $250 or more; lobbyists should not be involved in arranging gifts, and employer contributions of $25 or more must be reported as payments to influence.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a revised version of prior advice dated December 16, 1976; contains two memos (cover memo and substantive advice memo); no traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections, but substantive legal guidance is present in the second memorandum; revisions noted by underlining and strikeout (not visible in provided text).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the reporting obligations for state officials who receive gifts of money, food, wine, and other supplies from private sector donors in connection with the Western Association of State Land Commissions' annual convention?

CONCLUSION: Officials must report cumulative gifts totaling $25 or more from all donors combined, providing a description, value estimate, and donor list; disqualification from decisions applies only when an individual donor provides $250 or more; lobbyists should not be involved in arranging gifts, and employer contributions of $25 or more must be reported as payments to influence.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter providing guidance on gift reporting obligations for state officials receiving contributions from private donors for the Western Association of State Land Commissions convention, including thresholds for reporting ($25) and disqualification ($250) and restrictions on lobbyist involvement.
